prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `78e78cff`.
codex_response:
Implemented the new ARC2 task package for `78e78cff` in [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_78e78cff/__init__.py), [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_78e78cff/verifier.py#L4), and [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_78e78cff/generator.py#L80). The rule is: use the singleton-color cell only as the fill color, then fill row-wise background gaps defined by the other foreground color; empty rows inside the occupied band become full-width fills, and rows above/below reuse the nearest occupied boundary row’s gap pattern.

I discarded the `arc2_opus46_summary.json` hint and documented the mismatch in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_78e78cff/notes.md#L1), because its “vertical stripe above/below” claim is false for the official examples. The generator matches the family by explicitly constructing top/bottom boundary-gap profiles plus a full middle band, then deriving the sparse side-marker input from those latent gaps. No new primitive was needed.

Validation passed end to end: official examples `3/3`, generated examples `1000/1000`, and I visually checked the preview output. Artifacts are at [task.json](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/78e78cff/task.json), [originals.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/78e78cff/previews/originals.png), [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/78e78cff/previews/preview_01.png), [preview_02.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/78e78cff/previews/preview_02.png), and [preview_03.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/78e78cff/previews/preview_03.png).
